description:
You are acting as an observability engineer who has just received three raw metric
    files that were exported from a staging Prometheus instance.  
    All three files already exist inside  
    /home/user/observability/raw_metrics/ :

    1. /home/user/observability/raw_metrics/cpu_metrics.csv  
    2. /home/user/observability/raw_metrics/memory_metrics.csv  
    3. /home/user/observability/raw_metrics/http_requests.csv  

    Their exact, given contents are:

    ---- cpu_metrics.csv ----
    timestamp,cpu_usage_percent
    2024-06-01T10:00:00Z,45.3
    2024-06-01T11:00:00Z,55.1
    2024-06-01T12:00:00Z,49.8

    ---- memory_metrics.csv ----
    timestamp,mem_used_mb
    2024-06-01T10:00:00Z,7832
    2024-06-01T11:00:00Z,8124
    2024-06-01T12:00:00Z,7998

    ---- http_requests.csv ----
    timestamp,method,status_code,latency_ms
    2024-06-01T10:00:00Z,GET,200,123
    2024-06-01T10:01:00Z,POST,200,256
    2024-06-01T10:02:00Z,GET,500,789
    2024-06-01T11:15:00Z,GET,200,101
    2024-06-01T12:22:00Z,POST,404,303

    ------------------------------------------------------------------

    Your goal:
    1. Create the directory /home/user/observability/reports/ (if it does not already
       exist).
    2. From the raw CSVs, calculate a very small weekly roll-up that will be used by
       a Grafana “metrics-overview” dashboard.
    3. Write a new file called /home/user/observability/reports/weekly_system_metrics.log
       that contains exactly ten (10) lines, with *no* header or trailing blank line.

       The log file format (which the automated tests will check verbatim) must be:

           SUMMARY_TYPE|METRIC_NAME|VALUE

       Rules for formatting VALUE:
       • Integers appear with no decimal point.  
       • Floats must be shown with *exactly two* digits after the decimal point, even if
         those digits are “00”.  
       • Do not add thousands separators.

       The ten required lines, in *this precise order*, are:

         1. AVG|cpu_usage_percent|<two-decimal-float>
         2. MAX|cpu_usage_percent|<two-decimal-float>
         3. MIN|cpu_usage_percent|<two-decimal-float>
         4. AVG|mem_used_mb|<two-decimal-float>
         5. MAX|mem_used_mb|<integer>
         6. MIN|mem_used_mb|<integer>
         7. AVG|latency_ms|<two-decimal-float>
         8. MAX|latency_ms|<integer>
         9. MIN|latency_ms|<integer>
        10. COUNT|http_requests|<integer>

       Additional derived metric:
       • ERROR_RATE|http_requests|<two-decimal-float>  
         This is optional for you to compute, but if you do add it, make sure it is
         inserted as *line 11* (after the ten mandatory lines) and follows the exact
         same delimiter rules.

       Notes on the calculations:
       • AVG is the arithmetic mean across every numeric row in the CSV.  
       • ERROR_RATE = (non-2xx responses) / (total requests).  
       • COUNT is simply the number of rows in http_requests.csv.

    4. Keep the solution to 5–10 shell commands total (scripted or typed manually).

    5. After you are done, you should be able to run
          cat /home/user/observability/reports/weekly_system_metrics.log
       and see the correctly formatted summary.

    Place all intermediary scripts or helper files (if you decide to create any) inside
    /home/user/observability/scripts/ so we keep the workspace tidy.

    The automated evaluation will:
       • Confirm /home/user/observability/reports/ exists.  
       • Read /home/user/observability/reports/weekly_system_metrics.log and compare it
         byte-for-byte with the expected content.  
       • (If present) Verify the optional ERROR_RATE line.

    Do NOT print the expected final content yourself—just create the file with the
    right values.
truth:
The three raw metric files are pre-populated exactly as listed in the task.

    The correct roll-up values that the grader will look for are:

      AVG cpu_usage_percent   = (45.3 + 55.1 + 49.8) / 3  = 50.066666… => 50.07
      MAX cpu_usage_percent   = 55.1
      MIN cpu_usage_percent   = 45.3

      AVG mem_used_mb         = (7832 + 8124 + 7998) / 3  = 7984.666…  => 7984.67
      MAX mem_used_mb         = 8124
      MIN mem_used_mb         = 7832

      AVG latency_ms          = (123 + 256 + 789 + 101 + 303) / 5 = 314.4 => 314.40
      MAX latency_ms          = 789
      MIN latency_ms          = 101

      COUNT http_requests     = 5

      ERROR_RATE              = 2 non-2xx / 5 total = 0.4 => 0.40

    Therefore the file the agent must create is the single, exact-text file:

    Path: /home/user/observability/reports/weekly_system_metrics.log  
    Contents (newline-terminated, no extra blank line at the end):

        AVG|cpu_usage_percent|50.07
        MAX|cpu_usage_percent|55.10
        MIN|cpu_usage_percent|45.30
        AVG|mem_used_mb|7984.67
        MAX|mem_used_mb|8124
        MIN|mem_used_mb|7832
        AVG|latency_ms|314.40
        MAX|latency_ms|789
        MIN|latency_ms|101
        COUNT|http_requests|5

    If the agent chooses to output the optional error-rate line, it **must** be this
    exact 11th line:

        ERROR_RATE|http_requests|0.40

    The autograder will allow either 10 lines (without ERROR_RATE) or 11 lines (with it).
